title: BEM-919 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM919_WORKER_FULL_SYSTEM_WORKING_PROTOCOL_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-919 WORKER FULL SYSTEM WORKING PROTOCOL REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- Operator correction is valid. The protocol must cover the whole system, not only Telegram.
- Protocol v1.7 created a useful governance baseline and later remediation added deterministic pieces, but the system is not yet fully working as a live operational system.
- Claude audit findings remain mandatory: JSON-only objects, commit_sha/proof gap, no live consumer, self-validation, stale queues, architecture canon, event log completeness, GD/DIR prompts and re-audit.
- Several findings were partially remediated in BEM-907..BEM-913, but partial remediation is not final working state.
- Existing Telegram/Deno mechanism must be reused: ai-devops-telegram-curator-webhook, POST / Telegram webhook, autonomy endpoints, gpt-safe legacy endpoints, codex-task/status, codex-runner.yml.
- The seven listed mechanisms must be classified by status. Not all are equal and not all should be primary. Primary core is Telegram webhook + codex-task/status + codex-runner. Autonomy endpoints are support. gpt-safe is legacy compatibility. Production token/webhook/schedule/daemon is operator-gated.
- Telegram route is confirmed as Operator <-> Telegram webhook <-> EL-CUR-GD-001 <-> OBJ-GD-001 <-> Director/Worker through managed channel.
- Reports must use operator-selected periodicity with default 1 hour and must follow report canon.
Recommended protocol structure:
1. Executive summary and current verdict.
2. v1.7 plan vs fact analysis.
3. Claude findings and remediation status.
4. Operator remarks and design corrections.
5. Component status matrix.
6. Target working architecture.
7. Full roadmap BEM-920..BEM-928 with owners, acceptance criteria, proof and gates.
8. Autonomous vs operator-gated split.
9. Release readiness and Claude re-audit criteria.
Recommended roadmap:
- BEM-920: component inventory, SSOT freeze, archive/rudiment cleanup.
- BEM-921: independent proof hardening: CI/Git SHA, release manifest, Actions log evidence.
- BEM-922: Telegram operator workflow on existing webhook: parser, envelope, General Director routing, command grammar.
- BEM-923: operator gate for production token/secrets/webhook/schedule/daemon model.
- BEM-924: dispatch lifecycle: queued -> delivered -> processing -> result -> proof -> completed/operator_gate.
- BEM-925: object execution lifecycle: GD/DIR/Worker runners, state, inbox/outbox, role reports.
- BEM-926: managed channel, event log completeness, canonical report pipeline and 1-hour default config.
- BEM-927: E2E tests: Telegram mock command -> General Director -> dispatch -> worker -> proof -> canonical Telegram report.
- BEM-928: Claude re-audit package and release decision.
Autonomous scope:
- BEM-920, BEM-921 design and workflow files, BEM-922 mock/parser/envelope, BEM-924 lifecycle scripts, BEM-925 deterministic runners, BEM-926 logs/reports, BEM-927 mock E2E, BEM-928 package.
Operator gates:
- BEM-923: real token/secrets, production webhook mutation, new schedule trigger, always-on daemon, issue #31 write permission, real pilot/client/scope/metrics, paid API and LLM-agent runtime model.
Recommendation:
- Approve full-system protocol with notes.
- Status: FULL_SYSTEM_WORKING_PROTOCOL_APPROVED_WITH_OPERATOR_GATES.
No issue comments.
